pi_items.source: Verified via cross-reference: (1) task.json description lists 'New order' -> fulfillment@example.com, (2) verifier.py line 31 uses metadata.get('expected_new_order', 'fulfillment@example.com'), (3) consistent across all task files

Verified via cross-reference: (1) task.json description lists 'Cancelled order' -> accounting@example.com, (2) verifier.py line 32 uses metadata.get('expected_cancelled_order', 'accounting@example.com'), (3) consistent across all task files

Verified via cross-reference: (1) task.json description lists 'Failed order' -> sysadmin@example.com, (2) verifier.py line 33 uses metadata.get('expected_failed_order', 'sysadmin@example.com'), (3) consistent across all task files

Verified via cross-reference: (1) setup_task.sh line 15 sets DEFAULT_RECIPIENT='admin@example.com' as the initial state, (2) verifier.py line 34 uses metadata.get('forbidden_recipient', 'admin@example.com') to check admin email is not present in any field, (3) verifier.py lines 92-93 confirms admin email must be removed from all modified fields

privileged_info_summary: The task configures WooCommerce email notification recipients. Verified email recipients are: new orders should be sent to fulfillment@example.com, cancelled orders to accounting@example.com, and failed orders to sysadmin@example.com. The default admin@example.com recipient must be removed from all notification types. All metadata values are consistent across task description, verifier logic, and setup scripts.
